design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from 'Super vs Gemini|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Gemini|2026-07-06T14:14:18Z' -> selected architecture 'comparison-lab'. AIDA Check: Hero (Attention), proof + comparison grid (Interest), motion + field guide (Desire), CTA band (Action). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (primary) with system fallbacks. Hero Math Verification: max-width 1120px, clamp headline 42–72px to ensure 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid and bento use grid-auto-flow: dense. GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics, guarded if GSAP unavailable. Label Sweep & Button Check: black pill primary CTA, high-contrast secondary CTA verified.
headline: Super vs Gemini — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Gemini. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Gemini

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison for people deciding between Google Gemini and Super. Gemini is pushing agentic automation into macOS and the browser. Super focuses on durable computer‑use workflows with a reusable computer‑use cache that makes repeated work cheaper and more reliable.
